core_mechanism: classification of a driven dissipative system as occupying a distinct non-equilibrium phase, defined by a set of necessary order-parameter-like criteria (sustained free-energy throughput plus self-maintaining organizational closure) whose phase boundaries are calibrated against reference driven systems that lack closure
brief_reason: The abstract defines minimal self-maintaining far-from-equilibrium organization as a distinct non-equilibrium phase of matter and builds a diagnostic phase-space of necessary conditions (throughput, closure, information maintenance, regulated noise) whose boundaries are fixed by baseline driven systems such as Bénard convection.
